title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 340.320 - Practice as technician after revocation, penalty — application for renewal
text:
340.320.  Practice as technician after revocation, penalty — application for renewal. — 1.  Any person who practices as a veterinary technician after his or her certificate has been revoked pursuant to section 340.318 is in violation of sections 340.200 to 340.330 and subject to criminal prosecution as provided for under sections 340.200 to 340.330. Such criminal penalty shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any penalty or other discipline provided for under sections 340.200 to 340.330.
  2.  If a person is otherwise eligible to renew his or her certificate, such person may renew an expired certificate within two years of the date of expiration by submitting an application for renewal, payment of the renewal fee, payment of delinquent renewal fees and payment of a penalty fee as established by the board.  A certificate may not be renewed if two years have lapsed since the date the certificate expired.  Such holder of an expired certificate must make application for a new certificate.
(L. 1992 H.B. 878 § 60, A.L. 1999 S.B. 424, A.L. 2004 H.B. 869)
